description_long: This object is a simple grave plot featuring a rectangular, slightly raised base covered in what appears to be weathered, mossy stone or concrete. At one end of the plot stands a wooden Latin cross, aged and slightly tilted, serving as the primary grave marker. Adjacent to the cross is a small, upright wooden stake, possibly marking the grave's boundary or serving as an additional marker. The surface of the grave is uneven and shows signs of exposure to the elements, with patches of discoloration and moss. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative elements on the cross or the grave surface. The overall appearance suggests a modest, possibly older grave in a less formal cemetery or rural graveyard.
description: A modest grave plot with a weathered stone base, a wooden Latin cross, and an upright wooden stake, showing signs of age and exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden cross at the head of the grave and the upright wooden stake further down the plot.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the grave, with both the cross and the stake visible at opposite ends.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the cross and the stake, with the grave surface extending between them.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the cross and stake from the other side.
